title: 'No legal action against CJP protesters': Wangchuk sets condition to end hunger strike
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk will end his hunger strike if protesters face no legal action. He seeks assurance from the government regarding the CJP's march participants. Wangchuk also requested compensation for affected students and parliamentary discussion. He wants to return to his work and students after receiving this assurance. The activist will continue his fast indefinitely without this government guarantee.

title: 'I would have headbutted him!': Zlatan slams Paredes, asks why Spain players 'just watched'
content: Zlatan Ibrahimovic strongly criticised Argentine midfielder Leandro Paredes for his actions.  He stated he would have headbutted Paredes if he had been on the field.  Ibrahimovic also questioned the Spanish players' lack of response to the confrontation.  FIFA is investigating Paredes' conduct following the match.  Paredes later expressed pride in representing his country despite the loss.

title: Delhi HC seeks govt's response on pleas over alleged brutality during CJP march
content: The Delhi High Court issued a notice to the Delhi government on Wednesday.  This action follows petitions alleging police brutality against students during a recent march.  The court has directed the government to preserve all relevant digital records.  These records include CCTV footage and body camera recordings from the incident.  The matter has been scheduled for its next hearing on September eleventh.

title: Pentagon hit by AI's 'biggest problem' after Army burns a year’s tokens for one service
content: The US Army has depleted its entire annual budget for artificial intelligence processing tokens. This rapid consumption occurred within mere weeks of granting broad access to personnel. Military leaders have now reinstated strict usage limits across various departments. The shortage highlights concerns about generative AI overconsumption by organizations. This situation arises as cloud computing costs continue to escalate significantly.

title: 'No one has ever done this before': Harbhajan makes massive Sooryavanshi claim
content: Harbhajan Singh lauded Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's exceptional talent and fearless batting approach. He noted the youngster's confidence mirrors modern cricketers' evolving mindset. Harbhajan recalled Rahul Dravid's question about Sooryavanshi's first IPL ball. The former spinner shared Sachin Tendulkar's advice on maintaining focus and dedication. This guidance shapes Harbhajan's own counsel to aspiring young cricketers.

title: 'We literally have the paper': US says India trade deal could be signed in months
content: A significant India-US trade agreement is expected within three to four months. Negotiations are nearly finalized, with the text effectively complete and ready for signing. The primary remaining hurdle involves ongoing US trade investigations, not bilateral disagreements. Washington's Section 301 probes are nearing conclusion, which will allow progress. This agreement aims to expand market access and deepen economic ties between both nations.

title: Scientists heard bird sounds beneath floating Solar Farm in Netherlands
content: Floating solar farms in the Netherlands may be supporting local wildlife. Researchers observed bird sounds suggesting possible nesting under the solar panels. Underwater shelters installed beneath the panels attracted hundreds of fish and thousands of aquatic creatures. These structures developed into small ecosystems supporting life higher up the food chain. The findings suggest carefully designed farms could create new habitats for wildlife.

title: Trump wants FIFA chief Infantino as next UN secretary-general: Report
content: US President Donald Trump reportedly supports FIFA president Gianni Infantino for the UN secretary-general position. Trump believes Infantino possesses the ability to unite people globally and is a strong candidate. The two developed a closer relationship during the recent FIFA World Cup tournament. Infantino had previously presented Trump with FIFA's inaugural Peace Prize award. A candidate needs UN Security Council backing and General Assembly approval for the role.

title: Meet Hedy Lamarr, Hollywood icon and inventor called the mother of Wi-Fi
content: Hedy Lamarr, widely known for her illustrious acting career, also made significant strides in technology by inventing a communication system that used frequency hopping to encrypt radio signals in wartime. Her pioneering work paved the way for the development of contemporary innovations such as Wi-Fi and Bluetooth. Although her achievements were initially overlooked, she has since been recognized as a trailblazer in both the entertainment and tech industries.

title: Arizona boy, 14, helps save woman with dementia wandering in 103°F heat
content: Royal Cothrun, a fourteen-year-old boy, demonstrated extraordinary courage during a perilous heatwave by rescuing an elderly woman wandering aimlessly. With a swift instinct, he provided shade and facilitated her communication with her son, actions that were potentially lifesaving. The Arizona fire department is set to commend him for his heroic efforts, highlighting the critical risks associated with severe summer heat.

title: Meta's 'highest-paid' employee taunts Google’s AI model, says ‘Gemini who’
content: Meta's Alexandr Wang taunted Google's Gemini model after a leaderboard showed Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 performing better.  Google recently launched Gemini 3.6 Flash, claiming improved efficiency and quality over its predecessor.  Gemini 3.6 Flash shows higher precision in coding and better computer use capabilities.  It also outperforms the previous model in knowledge work and multimodal tasks.  This development highlights the ongoing competition in the artificial intelligence sector.

title: Running on rollovers: Pakistan's reserves get $27 billion foreign loans from Saudi, China
content: Pakistan secured approximately twenty-seven billion dollars in foreign funding last fiscal year. Saudi Arabia and China provided significant rollover support for existing loans. Fresh borrowing accounted for sixteen billion dollars of the total inflows. This foreign assistance primarily financed the budget and repaid external debt. Pakistan's exports declined by six percent during the same period.

title: Waited over 5 years for electricity connection, consumer wins Rs 40,000 compensation
content: The Delhi consumer commission found BSES Rajdhani Power guilty of service deficiency.  The power company took over five years to provide an electricity connection.  BSES could not prove it informed the consumer about application discrepancies.  The commission upheld compensation and litigation costs awarded by the district forum.  BSES must pay the awarded amount within forty-five days.

title: Jeetendra's Rs 4.25 lakh pali hill deal is now worth Rs 450 crore
content: Jeetendra purchased Bharat Bhushan's bungalow for a modest sum.  He later sold the Burma teak from the demolished structure for profit.  This initial investment laid the groundwork for his successful real estate career.  The property's value has since appreciated significantly over the years.  Today, the land alone is worth an estimated Rs 450 crore.

title: From Miami to Maui: Jeff Bezos' $800 million-plus real estate empire
content: Jeff Bezos has amassed a vast residential portfolio across the United States. His property collection spans from Washington state to South Florida and Hawaii. Bezos acquired multiple neighboring properties to expand his existing compounds. He also owns two luxury yachts, Koru and Abeona, which are assets afloat. This extensive real estate buying pattern reflects his significant wealth and investments.

title: Why did stock market crash today? Sensex ends over 700 points down, Nifty below 24,000
content: Pharmaceutical stocks were among the biggest drags on the market after Trump announced a phased tariff framework for imported generic medicines, giving drugmakers a two-year transition period before higher duties come into force.

title: India commissions INS Malvan to beef up sub-hunter fleet to counter China threat
content: The Indian Navy commissioned Malvan, its second Mahe-class vessel, at Karwar naval base. This new warship will bolster the nation's submarine hunter fleet in the Indian Ocean. The vessel is designed for shallow water anti-submarine operations and mine warfare. India is inducting sixteen specialized vessels to secure its extensive coastline. This move strengthens maritime security amid growing regional naval competition.

title: Kuldeep Yadav lands county deal after no-show in India vs England ODIs
content: Kuldeep Yadav has joined Yorkshire as an overseas player for the remainder of the English domestic season. The India spinner will play five One-Day Cup matches and three County Championship games. The move comes after he was left out of India's ODI series in England. Yorkshire believe Kuldeep's experience and left-arm wrist spin will strengthen their bowling attack.

title: Akshay Kumar used teleprompter in 'Toilet: Ek Prem Katha', says Sudhir Pandey
content: Veteran actor Sudhir Pandey discussed Akshay Kumar's use of teleprompters.  He noted Kumar relied on them for lengthy scenes in 'Toilet: Ek Prem Katha'.  Pandey also praised Kumar's significant acting improvement over the years.  Filmmaker Suneel Darshan previously shared similar insights about Kumar's prompts.  Kumar was last seen in the moderately successful film 'Welcome to the Jungle'.

title: 'Chapter is behind me': Lalit Modi says he'll return to India soon
content: Lalit Modi plans to return to India by year-end or early next year. This follows major legal relief from the SAFEMA Appellate Tribunal concerning a 2009 IPL case. The tribunal set aside most Enforcement Directorate findings and penalties related to foreign exchange transactions. Modi stated he fought the case for sixteen years and felt vindicated by the verdict.

title: Traffic jam! BCCI CoE braces for an influx of contracted, targeted India players
content: The BCCI Centre of Excellence is set for a hectic week as nearly a dozen contracted and targeted India players undergo fitness assessments and rehabilitation ahead of the Sri Lanka Tests. With injuries mounting and a packed international calendar looming, the team management is keen to ensure a fit and ready player pool.

title: Boiling point? Brent crude touches five week high, here’s what analysts expect next
content: Oil prices are climbing as geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran intensify. Analysts expect crude prices to remain within a specific range for now. Current market prices do not indicate a major structural disruption to global oil supplies. Investors are also closely monitoring upcoming crude oil inventory data. This data could influence near-term price direction for the commodity.

title: How tanks are being upgraded to stay relevant on the modern battlefield
content: Main battle tanks are transforming significantly due to new battlefield threats.  Armies are redesigning tanks with advanced protection and drone integration capabilities.  New systems detect and intercept incoming missiles and rockets effectively.  Tanks are becoming launch platforms for unmanned systems and digitally connected.  This evolution ensures tanks remain relevant in future conflicts.

title: Woman injured during CJP's Parliament march taken off ventilator; responding to treatment
content: A 21-year-old woman injured during a protest is now conscious and breathing independently. Doctors at RML Hospital confirmed her steady improvement and normal MRI scans. She was injured during police action at a Cockroach Janta Party protest on July 20. Protesters marched towards Parliament demanding the Education Minister's resignation over an alleged exam leak. Police used tear gas and lathi charge to disperse the crowd.

title: Suvendu counters Abhishek Banerjee's '2031 settlement' vow with Diamond Harbour dare
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari challenged Abhishek Banerjee to contest Diamond Harbour.  Adhikari questioned Banerjee's political standing and future prospects.  This exchange follows Banerjee's remarks at a recent Martyrs' Day rally.  Banerjee had vowed to settle political accounts with the BJP in 2031.  Diamond Harbour has been Banerjee's Lok Sabha constituency since 2014.

title: 'You let BJP, Cong speak, but not us': Akhilesh erupts in LS over NEET discussion
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav accused the Lok Sabha Speaker of unfair treatment. He stated his party was denied a chance to speak on the NEET paper leak. The government and Congress were allowed to present their views while his party was not. Yadav insisted the controversy concerned students and their future, not just politics. He defended protests outside the Prime Minister's residence due to the lack of House discussion.

title: CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march: Students recount beatings with ‘nail-fitted sticks’
content: Students returned to Jantar Mantar with injuries from Monday's police lathicharge.  Some protesters alleged police used lathis fitted with nails and screws.  Women police personnel were also accused of slapping female students during the incident.  A stampede-like situation occurred near Janpath metro station as protesters fled.  Despite injuries, students returned to the protest, stating the cause was larger than any organization.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' box office collection day 13: Ajay Devgn starrer crosses Rs 132 crore
content: Indra Kumar’s 'Dhamaal 4' continues to show solid resilience at the domestic box office. Pushed by its signature slapstick comedy and strong family appeal, the franchise sequel has comfortably sailed past the Rs. 100 crore net milestone in India and is now closing in on the Rs. 135 crore domestic net benchmark.  Despite losing IMAX and premium screens to Christopher Nolan's epic 'The Odyssey', 'Dhamaal 4' is holding its ground across mass circuits and single screens.

title: In 2023, Hindon entered villages. In 2026, villages have gone further into Hindon
content: New constructions are appearing on the Hindon river floodplain, three years after devastating floods. Residents have rebuilt homes and added structures, citing past government inaction. Land sharks are allegedly dumping debris and narrowing the river's course for plot sales. Environmental activists note ongoing construction activity despite official oversight. Authorities have conducted anti-encroachment drives, clearing some floodplain areas.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' cast fees revealed: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay tops list
content: Jana Nayagan's cast fees are revealed as the film nears its release date. Vijay leads the salary chart with a record payday, reportedly between Rs 220 crore and Rs 275 crore. Pooja Hegde, Bobby Deol, and Prakash Raj also received substantial remunerations for their roles. Director H Vinoth and composer Anirudh Ravichander earned impressive fees for their contributions.

title: Hollywood Hills to Malibu beachfront: Leonardo DiCaprio's multi-million dollar properties
content: Leonardo DiCaprio boasts a remarkable real estate portfolio that spans the United States and Belize. His assets feature a lavish compound in Hollywood Hills, a tranquil private island in Belize, chic apartments in New York City, and a sprawling estate in Palm Springs. Furthermore, DiCaprio has acquired a stunning Spanish Colonial home in Los Feliz, along with a picturesque beachfront property in Malibu and a stylish residence in Beverly Hills.

title: Tamil Nadu sanctions Rs 10.71 crore for climate-resilient hill town master plans
content: Tamil Nadu is preparing climate-resilient master plans for seven major hill towns. These plans aim to improve roads, parking, and waste management systems. They will also safeguard ecologically fragile regions from unplanned urban development. The government is using advanced mapping and drone surveys for accurate environmental assessments. This initiative seeks to create safer and more sustainable hill settlements for everyone.

title: Virat Kohli, Anushka Sharma spotted in Vrindavan barefoot after meeting Premanand Ji Maharaj
content: Virat Kohli and Anushka Sharma visited Premanand Maharaj's ashram in Vrindavan recently.  This spiritual trip followed Royal Challengers Bengaluru's IPL victory over Gujarat Titans.  The couple frequently seeks spiritual guidance from the Vrindavan-based leader.  Kohli reflected on his team's balance and depth after their IPL triumph.  He credited the squad's belief and execution for their successful campaign.

title: School holidays extended in J&K till July 26 as IMD forecasts heavy rain
content: Jammu and Kashmir has extended school holidays until July 26 for student safety.  Widespread rainfall is forecast for Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand over the coming days.  Authorities are closely monitoring the monsoon's intensification across the Himalayan region.  No statewide school closure orders have been issued in Himachal and Uttarakhand yet.  Students should check official notifications for any updates on school operations.

title: Trump’s 200% tariffs on pharma: Why India’s generic medicines could still remain competitive
content: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday announced the final stage of his pharmaceutical tariff plan, under which imported generic medicines will continue to enter the US without duties until August 1, 2028.

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Before breaking fast, Wangchuk seeks 'unequivocal assurance' that no young protester will face punitive, retaliatory legal action
content: Congress supports all demands raised by students, Modi must apologise to them, says Rahul; Delhi HC seeks Centre, police response on pleas over use of 'excessive' force during July 20 protest march

title: Tensions escalate in Vijayawada as BJP activists march to Congress office over NEET protests
content: BJP workers led by State president P.V.N. Madhav gathered outside Andhra Ratna Bhavan on Wednesday against the Congress’s Delhi sit-in over the alleged NEET-UG leak; the party alleged that three of its district leaders were injured, and the police detained some activists

title: World Brain Day: Mental health is as important as physical health, says Ballari District Health and Family Welfare Officer
content: Brain disorders should be identified early to prevent permanent disability, say doctors on World Brain Day

title: Bullet Train corridor being equipped with advanced electrification system: NHSRCL
content: The advanced 2×25 kV Overhead Traction System, being introduced in India for the very first time, is designed to provide a stable and uninterrupted power supply required for trains operating at speeds of up to 320 kmph, NHSRCL officials said

title: Nothing can shame people unfazed by Ram temple donation theft: Allahabad High Court
content: Justice Sreedharan simultaneously criticised the systemic corruption plaguing Indian institutions, noting that the recent “theft” of donations at the Ram temple represents the “nadir of Indians’ integrity”

title: Mittaganahalli landfill, sitting on 287 crore litres of leachate, leaks into Srinivasapura Lake
content: The landfill is located at a higher elevation than the lake, and the leachate flows through a natural waterway before entering drain lines created for a nearby private layout, which then joins a natural drain, ultimately ending at the lake inlet

title: ABVP claims Jantar Mantar protest was ‘hijacked’, presses govt. to form panel on exam reforms
content: In a statement, the student wing of RSS denounces the clashes during the demonstration, saying the legitimate concerns of students should be addressed through dialogue, transparency and institutional reforms

title: Opposition using students' protests as political tool to serve interests: Rajnath Singh
content: The Defence Minister alleged that some Opposition leaders are displaying a "manufactured sense of anger" to confuse and mislead people, especially the youth.

title: This heinous act will not go unpunished: Jammu and Kashmir L-G on Anantnag terrorist attack
content: The L-G said that he spoke to the Director General of Police (DGP) Nalin Prabhat to take stock of the situation

title: Lok Sabha suspends TMC’s Kalyan Banerjee for using ‘unsavoury’ language against women MPs
content: As soon as the House assembled at 2 p.m. after the second adjournment of the day, Krishna Prasad Tenneti, who was chairing the proceedings, referred to a complaint filed by members with the speaker against Mr. Banerjee and said his remarks had hurt the dignity of the House.

title: Supreme Court asks States, UTs to consider raising retirement age of judicial officers from 60 to 61 years
content: A Bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V. Mohana passed the direction while hearing a petition seeking uniform enhancement of the retirement age of district judiciary judges from 60 to 62 years across the country

title: What caused the NHPC tunnel disaster in Sikkim’s Namchi? | Explained
content: Methane trapped inside rock is suspected to have triggered the fatal blast. But how does this happen?

title: Clash erupts between BJP & Cong workers in Hyderabad; Telangana BJP chief Ramchander Rao detained
content: Telangana BJP leaders and cadre were taken into preventive custody by the police while proceeding towards Gandhi Bhavan to stage a protest against Rahul Gandhi’s ‘dharna’ near Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s residence

title: Visakhapatnam airport commercial operations to shift to Bhogapuram around August 15
content: The 3,800-metre Code 4E runway at Bhogapuram, among the longest in India, will handle wide-body aircraft such as the Boeing 777 and the Airbus A330 when commercial flights shift from INS Dega around mid-August

title: Killai becomes Tamil Nadu’s first fully operational climate-resilient village
content: Killai has transformed a climate-vulnerable coastal settlement into a working model of how ecosystem restoration, renewable energy, and community-led action can jointly address cyclones, sea-level rise, saline intrusion, coastal erosion, and biodiversity loss

title: Pellet guns, shock batons used against protesters in Delhi; police deny
content: Shaikh Irshad Mansoori suffered pellet injuries, the first known case of security forces using pellet guns in Delhi; police deny use, say public must “verify facts”

title: Rahul Gandhi misused LoP privileges, protest outside PM's residence childish: BJP
content: Congress leaders, led by Rahul Gandhi and party president Mallikarjun Kharge, had staged a dharna outside the Prime Minister's residence on July 21, demanding PM Modi's resignation over the police action against students protesting the alleged NEET paper leak issue

title: WhatsApp weather alerts for people in Telangana villages under IMD’s Mission Mausam
content: Critical information on heavy rain, heatwaves, lightning and other weather events to reach villages faster

title: PMK founder Ramadoss withdraws suit against son Anbumani
content: Dr. Ramadoss had filed the suit seeking a declaration that he was the party president and an injunction restraining Dr. Anbumani from using the PMK’s name, flag, and symbol

title: Delhi HC seeks Centre, police response on pleas alleging ‘excessive’ force during July 20 CJP protest march
content: The Bench also directed authorities to preserve all relevant records in relation to the police action, including CCTV camera footage and any videography

title: SC declines interim stay on Speaker’s decision to approve Uddhav Sena MPs’ ‘merger’ with Shinde faction
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Arvind Sawant, who filed the petition, argued that the purported merger is ‘unconstitutional’ and ‘perverse’; the Bench, however, declined to give any such assurance and posted the matter for hearing after two weeks

title: Nagercoil custodial death revives memories of the 1996 prison killing that shook Tamil Nadu
content: While Lingam, a kabaddi player, was lodged in the Nagercoil District Sub-Jail, a gang of over 30 people broke into the prison from the outside and murdered him in 1996. He was decapitated, and his head was dumped at the Meenachipuram bus stand

title: Stable India-China ties will help build ‘multi-polar Asia’ and ‘multi-polar world’: Jaishankar
content: External Affairs Minister discusses India-China normalisation with Wang Yi, raises safety of Indian seafarers with Sergey Lavrov, and joins Quad talks backing a free and open Indo-Pacific

title: Chhattisgarh liquor scam case: Supreme Court rejects pleas of ED, State against grant of bail to Chaitanya Baghel
content: According to the ED, the liquor ‘scam’ in the State, was orchestrated between 2019 and 2022 when Chhattisgarh was governed by former Chief Minister Bhupesh Baghel

title: Bihar Police fire tear gas shells, lathi-charge protesting students demanding Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation
content: The protesters had gathered under the banner of the All India Students’ Association, an affiliate of the CPI(ML) Liberation, and they faced the police action near Gandhi Maidan

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ director H Vinoth interview: On Thalapathy Vijay’s support during controversies
content: Filmmaker H Vinoth speaks about his upcoming film ‘Jana Nayagan,’ starring Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay, the unwavering support he received from the former star during the turbulent months prior to its release, what success means for a politically-charged film that unfortunately leaked online, and more

title: AIADMK submits clarification to Speaker on anti-defection petition
content: Earlier, the Speaker had sought certain clarifications from Edappadi Palaniswami after receiving written explanations from former MLAs Maragatham Kumaravel, S. Jayakumar, P. Sathyabama, and Esaki Subaya in response to notices issued to them under the anti-defection law

title: ‘Shown that this fight is bigger than any one person’: CJP urges Sonam Wangchuk to end fast, says movement will continue
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke also questioned PM Modi's silence on the issue even as Sonam Wangchuk’s fast entered day 25

title: Fake Cabinet post scam targeting Kerala MLA: Cyber police identify prime suspect in New Delhi
content: Investigation team, which zeroed in on the suspect during visit to national capital as part of intensified probe, says many individuals are involved in the racket, with several of them based in New Delhi

title: Cockroach Janta Party protests | Collection of The Hindu’s ground reports
content: Tens of thousands of young protesters took to the streets of New Delhi, clashing with police who used tear gas and batons to push them back

title: Air India extends Tel Aviv-Delhi flight suspension until September-end
content: The suspension of Air India flights has caused major worries among more than 40,000 Indians living in Israel who wish to travel to India

title: Protesters gather at Shivaji Park in Mumbai; around 300 detentions reported on July 20
content: Police have registered eight FIRs across Mumbai stations naming over 900 accused, with the highest count exceeding 600 at Shivaji Park Police Station

title: Abolition of NEET only solution to students’ issues: DMK leader Stalin
content: He said that more than any other demand, NEET abolition should be at the heart of the student protests in Delhi

title: TVK government must cooperate with Centre on new airport in Tamil Nadu: BJP
content: State BJP chief Nainar Nagenthran said that political reasons should not compromise the development of Tamil Nadu

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 3 updates: Government says ready to discuss paper leak; Pradhan must resign first, says Kharge
content: Union Ministers Kiren Rijiju and J.P. Nadda said that the government was ready to discuss paper leaks and other issues in Parliament; Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha Mallikarjun Kharge says that Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan must resign for a fair discussion to take place; logjam continues and repeated adjournments rock both Houses

title: Sreenarayanaguru Open University to get permanent campus as Kerala govt sanctions ₹90 crore
content: Higher Education Minister Roji M. John says the university, which presently operates from rented premises in Kollam, will have its own state-of-the-art four-storey campus spread across 16,864 sq. m.

title: From Potti Sriramulu to Sonam Wangchuk: Hunger strikes that changed India’s politics
content: From Potti Sriramulu’s statehood fast to Sonam Wangchuk’s agitation, here’s how six hunger strikes reshaped India’s politics, policies and public discourse.

title: BLOs should not insist on hardcopies if Enumeration Forms were submitted online, says Telangana CEO
content: BLOs should also not insist on Aadhaar cards for submission of EFs, says Telangana Chief Electoral Officer

title: Kerala CM says former CPI(M)-led LDF govt had agreed to appoint non-Muslim members on Waqf Board
content: V.D. Satheesan alleges that Opposition Leader Pinarayi Vijayan is trying to ‘foment communal sentiments' against Congress-led UDF govt

title: Green corridor set up to transport brain-dead Kollam councillor’s organs to Thiruvananthapuram
content: Liver, kidneys, and corneas of Kollam Corporation councillor B. Ajith Kumar to give a new lease of life to five

title: Before ‘Jana Nayagan’: when an 18-year-old Vijay walked the halls of Loyola College
content: Vijay’s maiden venture as a solo hero, Naalaiya Theerpu, helmed by S.A. Chandrasekhar, did not set the box office on fire; even as the audience response remained muted, the actor was noticed, and he won the Best New Face Actor award from Cinema Express

title: Mylatti BESS project to be commissioned in October, says Power Minister Sunny Joseph
content: The Minister directed officials and implementing agencies to ensure the projects are completed within the scheduled time frame

title: Kerala BDS student death: HC directs investigating officer, special court to produce documents in Dr. Ram’s arrest
content: HC observes that 'serious allegations’ have been raised against investigating officer in the release of the accused and expresses suspicion that top officials were involved in it to ‘save’ Dr. Ram from detention

title: US-India Trade Deal Could Be Signed In 3-4 Months, US Official Says
content: Washington and New Delhi have been negotiating a bilateral trade agreement to expand market access and lower trade barriers as part of efforts to deepen economic ties.

title: Adani Power Q1 Profit Jumps 47% To Rs 4,867 Crore
content: Adani Power said that consolidated power sale volume was higher by 16.9 per cent at 28.8 billion unit (BU) in Q1 FY27 compared to 24.6 BU in Q1 FY26, on the back of higher operating capacity and strong power demand growth.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Says Will End Fast If Assured Of No Action Against Students
content: In the absence of such an assurance, Wangchuk stressed he would be forced to continue his fast indefinitely.

title: Adani Green Energy Q1 Capacity Rises 27%, EBITDA At Record High
content: The company's operational renewable energy capacity increased to 20,142 MW from 15,815 MW a year earlier, reflecting a 27 per cent year-on-year growth, according to its stock exchange filing.

title: US Plans To Announce Nuclear Agreement With Saudi Arabia Amid Iran Tensions
content: The move comes as Washington fights a war with Iran that began in part over US concerns with Tehran's own nuclear program -- an issue that remains a key point of contention during now-stalled talks between the two foes.

title: 'Will Return To India By Early Next Year': Lalit Modi After Tribunal Verdict
content: Referring to the ruling, Lalit Modi, who lives abroad, said his 16-year-long legal fight has come to fruition.

title: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: "I Want To Live, But Not At Cost of Youth," Says Sonam Wangchuk
content: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: The huge protest over the NEET paper leak are continuing for the third straight day today.

title: Meta Lawsuit Shows That If AI Fires You, Proving It Is The Hard Part
content: Workers who claim to be targeted for layoffs because of disabilities or medical leave, often cannot muster the evidence of wrongdoing necessary to quickly secure a win in court.

title: 15 People Injured After Two Trams Collide On Rotterdam's Erasmus Bridge
content: Bridge's webcam footage showed a tram steaming into the back of another, sending debris flying across the lanes of traffic and damaging both trams.

title: Who Is Mykhailo Drapaty? All About Ukraine's New Army Chief
content: The 43-year-old Drapaty replaces Oleksandr Syrsky, 60, an accomplished military strategist who was long criticised for Soviet-style practices and not caring about human losses.

title: Iran-US War News Live Updates: Israel-Lebanon Talks To Be Held In Italy On August 4
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

title: Indigenous Shallow-Water Warship INS Malvan Commissioned By Indian Navy
content: INS Malvan epitomises the vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' with over 80% of its equipment made in India.

title: Video: BJP, Congress Workers Clash In Patna, Cops Resort To Lathi-Charge
content: Visuals showed both sides using lathis and attacking each other with stones during the clash. Several workers were injured on both sides.

title: Ebola Outbreak In Congo Reaches Nearly 1000 Deaths
content: As of Sunday, 2,473 cases have been recorded in the latest outbreak and 999 people have died, says latest update from Congo's Ministry of Health.

title: AI Firm Anthropic Donates $20 Million To Support AI Regulation Lobbying
content: The donation is an attempt by the AI sector to increase its influence over US policy, as the federal government considers more oversight of the sector.

title: Louvre Apollo Gallery Reopens After $102 Million Crown Jewels Theft
content: The public can tour the majestic room and view its gilded ceilings and Greek frescoes en route to the nearby Mona Lisa, but they won't see any crown jewels or empty display cases.

title: OpenAI Says AI Models Went Rogue Triggering Unusual Breach During Testing
content: OpenAI's advanced models escaped containment, reach the internet, and break into Hugging Face while being tested in a controlled environment.

title: French Modelling Scout Linked To Epstein Found Dead At Home Near Paris
content: Under investigation in France, Daniel Siad, 69, was one of several French men accused of aiding Epstein to traffic and abuse women.

title: The 5,000-year-old mystery behind why every clock moves clockwise
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world.
